info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Appointment Scheduling API
info.description: The Appointment Scheduling API facilitates the creation of appointments for users, allowing businesses to manage their scheduling needs efficiently. This API enables users to book, modify, and confirm appointments with specific resources or personnel. Ideal for service-oriented businesses, such as clinics or salons, it streamlines appointment management, enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency. Developers can integrate this API to provide real-time appointment functionalities in their applications.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to create a new appointment by providing the necessary details. Ensure all required fields are filled out to successfully create an appointment.

requestBody.description: Request body for creating a new appointment. This should include all necessary details such as the date, time, and participant information.

200.description: Request completed successfully; appointment was created.

400.description: The request parameters are invalid; check the provided data.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server; please try again later.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the response was generated.

status.description: The status code of the response.

error.description: An error message if the request failed.

message.description: A message providing additional information about the response.
message.example: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The path of the request that was processed.

trace.description: A tracing identifier for debugging purposes.

suggestion.description: Suggested actions for the user based on the response.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

example.message: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving appointment details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

service.description: The name of the service that processed the request.

errorCode.description: An error code associated with the response.

message.description: A message providing further information about the response details.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred, formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with the error.

error.description: A short description of the error type.

message.description: A detailed message describing the error and potential reasons for it.
message.example: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The path of the API endpoint that resulted in the error.

trace.description: A trace identifier for debugging purposes.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for resolving the error or improving the request.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the error response returned by the system when an appointment operation fails. It includes details about the error, suggested actions, and additional contextual information.

example.message: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving appointment details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

service.description: The service that reported the error.

errorCode.description: A specific error code related to the issue.

message.description: A detailed message provided by the service.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Request

appointmentDate.description: Date of the appointment in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

appointmentTime.description: Time of the appointment in 24-hour format (HH:MM).

department.description: Department where the appointment will take place.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the request body for creating a new appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with the error.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message explaining the error.
message.example: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

trace.description: A trace or additional error information for debugging.

suggestion.description: Possible actions or suggestions for the user to resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing an error response for the appointment operation.

example.message: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving appointment details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates which HTTP headers might change in response to this request, allowing caching mechanisms to understand how responses vary.

Location.description: Provides the URL of the resource that was created or modified as a result of the request. This header is often used for redirection purposes.

content-type.description: Specifies the media type of the resource being returned in the response. This is important for clients to understand how to interpret the response data.
